book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.

ccel.title: ANF06. Fathers of the Third Century: Gregory Thaumaturgus, Dionysius the Great, Julius Africanus, Anatolius, and Minor Writers, Methodius, Arnobius

source.title: ANF06. Fathers of the Third Century: Gregory Thaumaturgus, Dionysius the Great, Julius Africanus, Anatolius, and Minor Writers, Methodius, Arnobius

title: Chapter XXXII.

source.title: ANF06. Fathers of the Third Century: Gregory Thaumaturgus, Dionysius the Great, Julius Africanus, Anatolius, and Minor Writers, Methodius, Arnobius

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Moreover, as to this word which is written in the Gospel, “Ye are of your father the devil,” and so forth, we say in brief that there is a devil working in us, whose aim it has been, in the strength of his own will, to make us like himself.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: For all the creatures that God made, He made very good; and He gave to every individual the sense of free-will, in accordance with which standard He also instituted the law of judgment.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: To sin is ours, and that we sin not is God’s gift, as our will is constituted to choose either to sin or not to sin.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: And this you doubtless understand well enough yourself, Manes; for you know that, although you were to bring together all your disciples and admonish them not to commit any transgression or do any unrighteousness, every one of them might still pass by the law of judgment.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: And certainly whosoever will, may keep the commandments; and whosoever shall despise them, and turn aside to what is contrary to them, shall yet without doubt have to face this law of judgment.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Hence also certain of the angels, refusing to submit themselves to the commandment of God, resisted His will; and one of them indeed fell like a flash of lightning upon the earth, while others, harassed by the dragon, sought their felicity in intercourse with the daughters of men, and thus brought on themselves the merited award of the punishment of eternal fire.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: And that angel who was cast down to earth, finding no further admittance into any of the regions of heaven, now flaunts about among men, deceiving them, and luring them to become transgressors like himself, and even to this day he is an adversary to the commandments of God.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: The example of his fall and ruin, however, will not be followed by all, inasmuch as to each is given liberty of will.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: For this reason also has he obtained the name of devil, because he has passed over from the heavenly places, and appeared on earth as the disparager of God’s commandment.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: But because it was God who first gave the commandment, the Lord Jesus Himself said to the devil, “Get thee behind me, Satan;” and, without doubt, to go behind God is the sign of being His servant.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: And again He says, “Thou shalt worship the Lord thy God, and Him only shalt thou serve.”

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Wherefore, as certain men were inclined to yield obedience to his wishes, they were addressed in these terms by the Saviour: “Ye are of your father the devil, and the lusts of your father ye will do.”

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: And, in fine, when they are found to be actually doing his will, they are thus addressed: “O generation of vipers, who hath warned you to flee from the wrath to come?

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Bring forth therefore fruits meet for repentance.”

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: From all this, then, you ought to see how weighty a matter it is for man to have freedom of will.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: However, let my antagonist here say whether there is a judgment for the godly and the ungodly, or not.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Manes said: There is a judgment.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Archelaus said: I think that what we have said concerning the devil contains no small measure of reason as well as of piety.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: For every creature, moreover, has its own order; and there is one order for the human race, and another for animals, and another for angels.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Furthermore, there is but one only inconvertible substance, the divine substance, eternal and invisible, as is known to all, and as is also borne out by this scripture: “No man hath seen God at any time, save the only begotten Son, which is in the bosom of the Father.”

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: All the other creatures, consequently, are of necessity visible,—such as heaven, earth, sea, men, angels, archangels.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: But if God has not been seen by any man at any time, what consubstantiality can there be between Him and those creatures?

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Hence we hold that all things whatsoever have, in their several positions, their own proper substances, according to their proper order.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: You, on the other hand, allege that every living thing which moves is made of one, and you say that every object has received like substance from God, and that this substance is capable of sinning and of being brought under the judgment; and you are unwilling to accept the word which declares that the devil was an angel, and that he fell in transgression, and that he is not of the same substance with God.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Logically, you ought to do away with any allowance of the doctrine of a judgment, and that would make it clear which of us is in error.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: If, indeed, the angel that has been created by God is incapable of falling in transgression, how can the soul, as a part of God, be capable of sinning?

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: But, again, if you say that there is a judgment for sinning souls, and if you hold also that these are of one substance with God; and if still, even although you maintain that they are of the divine nature, you affirm that, notwithstanding that fact, they do not keep the commandments of God, then, even on such grounds, my argument will pass very well, which avers that the devil fell first, on account of his failure to keep the commandments of God.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: He was not indeed of the substance of God.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: And he fell, not so much to do hurt to the race of man, as rather to be set at nought by the same.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: For He “gave unto us power to tread on serpents and scorpions, and over all the strength of the enemy.”
